title: Antibody Purification (small scale)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9m5mq","text":"This protocol described a easy procedure to purify anti-sera in small scale batch. Protein A or protein G beads can be used depending on the immunoglobulin types.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Centrifuge anti-sera @ 4ºC,14,000g,10min. This step is to remove particulates in the serum.
(Optional) Filter through 0.2um filter. This step is to remove lipids in the serum.
Apply protein A or protein G beads gently to Bio-Rad empty gravity column. anti-sera:beads slurry=1:1(v/v)
Set up the column on a vacuum manifold. Equilibrate column with 10 volumes of ice cold Binding Buffer. Use vacuum to pack beads tightly. Binding Buffer: 1xPBS
Dilute anti-sera with 2 volumes of Binding Buffer.
Let diluted anti-sera go through the column by gravity. No vacuum here.
Collect flow through and repeat step 6 once.
Wash column with 10 bed volumes of ice-cold Binding Buffer.
Elute antibody with 5 bed volumes of room temperature Elution Buffer. Elution Buffer: 100mM citric acid,PH3.0 for protein A beads; 100mM Glycine-HCL,PH2.7 for protein G beads.
Fill up each collecting tube with 200 μl 1M Tris-HCl (PH9.0).
Collect 1ml of flow through in each collecting tube. Save for antibody activity test. The PH9.0 Tris-HCl in the collecting tube will take the PH of flow through to around 7.
Re-equilibrate column with 10 bed volumes of Binding Buffer. Store Column at refrigerator for later use.
creator: Vivian Liu
